Tanner: Hey everybody. Tanner Sheldon here and I am going to show you guys how to run a
virtual group, workshop or mastermind, just in a few easy steps, just for those that are
beginning and want to maybe tap into a virtual group before they go ahead and tackle
an in-person group.
First things first, why a virtual group, why a virtual workshop and why a virtual
mastermind? A few things, first thing: lower ticket prices for beginners. If you're not
ready to drop in $5000 for your students, $10,000, $25,000, all the way up to $250,000
like Dean and Tony do, this is a lower ticket price for your students. It's also easier to
create and manage compared to an in-person one, it's easier for your students to
commit to a weekend at home on the computer or ongoing help on a computer, rather
than spending three days away a couple times a year.
You also may be more comfortable for your first event, to do it on camera, rather than
in front of a room. The cost of the event will be much cheaper for the expert, compared
to if they do it in-person when you gotta spend a little bit more money. All of those are
great reasons as why a virtual would be a good step in the right direction for you. In-
person events are still amazing, unless your group connect on a deeper level, 'cause
they're with each other, you can charge a higher price point and you can over-deliver
even more. A virtual one is great. We have a few different examples coming up, so let's
go there.
Here are some different types of virtual groups, workshops or masterminds. First is a
virtual group. This can be something like Dean's inner circle or Dean's underground
millionaire mastermind. These are live trainings, once a month for about an hour going
over various topics, that's his inner circle, his underground millionaire mastermind is
more of a mastermind themed group, but at the end of the day, it's still a group, it's a
little different than a workshop or a mastermind. This is a beginner group where people
come for something new, something exciting, not something that's gonna necessarily
break that bank at $10 grand a month or $10 grand a year kind of thing. Our prices in
the past, have ranged from $7.00 to $97.00 a month or a flat fee of $500. Ours comes
with a private Facebook group, where everyone gets to know each other, it's really
awesome, the engagement in there is amazing and I do know personally that Dean's had
over a million dollars with his $7.00 group, his $47 and $97 group, all are amazing, it just
has different types of students involved in each one.
Just to give you an example, here is our underground millionaire mastermind Facebook
group that has over 8000 members in it, which is insanely engaged group. Everyone's
always commenting, supporting, everything and then Dean just really goes in a couple
times a month with some live videos, some posts, it's really powerful. Then this is our
inner circle group, which has over 3500 members. This is a little more expensive and this
is where Dean meets with them once a month live and does some really cool stuff.
Those are just examples of some of the virtual groups you can run and then virtual
workshop.
A workshop is a little different, if you think of a group as a handful of people to a lot of
people wanting to learn from the leader at the room, who's kind of there, kind of not.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

He's kind of putting people together and coming in every once in a while to check in and
then a virtual workshop is a little different. A workshop is where one person is kind of
delivering their knowledge to the group. It's more hands-on. I threw a couple of
examples in, like a hair stylist could take a day and do a virtual training on how he or she
does a specific hair technique, a specific dye, specific extensions, et cetera. That's more
one to many or an ex MLB baseball player who could spend the day showing the perfect
hitting techniques, it's more one person delivering their expertise to many people.
We've done ones in the past where we have a real estate expert who has some hand
holding with people on calling leads, closing sales, et cetera. Even Russel Brunson, who
is absolutely amazing has a workshop that he does in-person where it's more hands-on
and you teach people how to create and execute funnel building, but you could do the
same exact thing virtually. It's just one person kinda leading many and when it comes to
virtual masterminds, which is our next thing, mastermind is usually reserved for when a
collective genius of the group, helps every member go to the next level. It's really just,
the tide rises all boats. Everyone in there is really delivering to the value of the room,
making everyone scale and grow faster. It's one of the most powerful things in the
world.
This is something like, let's say, the highest level online marketers meet. They talk about
their biggest needle-movers, obstacles, opportunities, things they can do to go to the
next level or maybe it's a top 25 of all the Dentists in America getting together to discuss
new techniques, how to build their business. Virtual mastermind is very much like an in-
person one. There's a lot of questions, you can even raise your hand virtually. There's
chat, there's downloadable worksheets. It's very much ran like an in-person one, which
is really powerful.
The next thing I'm gonna do is go into the software behind your virtual event. The
software behind your virtual event is going to be Zoom. A lot of you may be familiar with
Zoom. I personally use Zoom all the time, truly amazing. That's what we recommend
people use for their online group, workshop or mastermind, just 'cause it's flexible, it's
very affordable. They even have a free version and it's really the lowest barrier of entry
for you to get into your first virtual event. Zoom is what we recommend.
Why Zoom? I just took some information from their website, just because they can
explain it just as good, if not better than me, but they have a really simplified video
conferencing and it works on all the devices, which is amazing. It's built for modern
teams, so you can communicate really easily. There's no complex algorithm that you
have to do to get a Zoom event going. Customers who switch to Zoom report an
increase of performance, trust and engagement, which I found was really good and they
had some great stats on that, compared to maybe using some of the other companies
out there. It's very modern, it's very thoughtful to mobile. It works really good on
phones, so if any of your attendees can't attend on a computer, they can attend on their
phone. It has a great Zoom chat, it keeps your participants engaged, 'cause there's a lot
of cool stuff they can do, like literally, you can virtually raise your hand when the person
asks questions, you can push out downloads, all sorts of cool stuff. It makes assistance
easy, has great audio and it integrates with a lot of stuff, which is really great.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
